title: Labour's Changing Brexit Plan
summary: The Labour Party in the UK is changing its ideas about Brexit, which was when Britain left the European Union. Chancellor Rachel Reeves said Brexit hurt the economy, so Labour wants to work more closely with Europe to help Britain grow. They don't want to rejoin the EU fully, but they aim to make rules more similar to Europe's in areas like food and farming to reduce checks and boost trade. This shift started after Labour lost an election in 2019 and now, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer, they believe a better relationship with Europe can fix economic problems. Some Labour members, like Sadiq Khan, want to go further and rejoin parts of the EU, but the party's main plan is to align rules where it helps Britain.

What did Labour lose in 2019 that made them change?

background_read: Brexit happened in 2020 when the UK left the European Union, a group of countries in Europe that work together. This caused changes in trade and rules. The Labour Party is one of the main political parties in the UK, and they are now trying to improve the economy by working more closely with Europe without fully rejoining.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Labour Party, including Chancellor Rachel Reeves and Prime Minister Keir Starmer
WHAT: Labour is shifting its Brexit focus to work more closely with Europe by aligning rules to help the economy
WHY: Because Brexit hurt the economy, and Labour wants to fix this by improving the relationship with Europe

perspectives.description: Believes Brexit damaged the economy and wants to align rules with Europe to boost growth without rejoining fully
